'''Valera''' was an astronomical object<ref name="Planets">''[[Planets of the Galaxy, Volume Three]]''</ref> located in the [[Valera system/Legends|Valera system]], a part of the [[Southern Core]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]].<ref name="EAOC" /> At some point between [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] and [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="Dates">''[[Planets of the Galaxy, Volume Three]]'', which is set before the events of [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']], establishes that the [[Valera uprising|uprising]] on Valera took place at some point following the [[Declaration of a New Order|establishment]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Since ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places the latter in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] and the former in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], the uprising must have occurred at some point between those two dates.</ref> an [[Valera uprising|uprising]] against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] took place on Valera. Although the insurrection was quelled by the [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Imperial Star Destroyer]] ''[[Stalker/Legends|Stalker]]'' under the [[Commanding officer/Legends|command]] of [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Akal Zed]], the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial forces]] lost several support [[Starship/Legends|vessels]] during the fighting due to flawed planning on Zed's part.<ref name="Planets" />

==Behind the scenes==
Valera was first mentioned in the [[1993]] sourcebook ''[[Planets of the Galaxy, Volume Three]]'', which was authored by [[John Terra]] for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="Planets" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Valera [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the celestial body Valera, within grid square L-13.<ref name="TEA" />
